name: plan-feature
description: Plan a new feature with analysis, design, and implementation steps. Use when the user asks to plan a feature or run /plan-feature.
capabilities: Gather feature requirements through clarifying questions
Analyze existing codebase for patterns and integration points
Design solution across backend, frontend, and cross-cutting concerns
Create ordered implementation tasks with dependencies
Save plans to .claude/plans/ directory as markdown files
Present design decisions and wait for user approval
author: DavidOpDeBeeck
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based skill containing only markdown documentation for feature planning workflows. All 48 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: backticks flagged as shell execution are markdown formatting, 'hash' references in metadata are data integrity fields, and 'weak crypto' flags target legitimate terminology. No executable code, network calls, or file system modifications beyond normal AI assistant operations.
valueStatement: Software teams need structured approaches to plan new features, but manually gathering requirements and designing architecture takes significant time. This skill provides a systematic workflow for analyzing requirements, exploring existing codebase patterns, and creating actionable implementation plans saved to persistent markdown files.
userTitle: Plan new features with structured analysis and design
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files without user approval
Does not access external services or APIs
Does not generate tests or implementation code directly
Does not perform build or deployment operations

useCases.title: Design Feature Architecture
useCases.description: Analyze codebase structure and design clean architectural solutions before implementation begins

useCases.title: Break Down Features
useCases.description: Convert high-level feature requests into atomic, testable tasks ordered by dependencies

useCases.title: Document Technical Plans
useCases.description: Create persistent markdown plans that capture requirements, design decisions, and implementation roadmap

promptTemplates.title: Complex Feature Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to plan a complex feature. Ask me clarifying questions to understand the requirements first.

promptTemplates.title: Feature Extension
promptTemplates.prompt: Plan how to extend the existing [feature name] with [new capability]. Analyze the current implementation first.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Feature Scoping
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /plan-feature to create an implementation plan for: [feature name]. Focus on affected modules and key tasks.

bestPractices: Start with clear feature names and acceptance criteria before invoking the skill
Review the generated plan and provide feedback before implementation begins
Use the saved markdown plans as reference documentation for future maintenance
antiPatterns: Invoking the skill without first defining the feature scope or goal
Skipping clarifying questions when requirements are ambiguous
Proceeding to implementation without user approval of the plan

faq.question: What tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: Compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Uses TodoWrite for task lists and file writing capabilities.

faq.question: How detailed are the implementation tasks?
faq.answer: Tasks are atomic and testable, broken down by module and layer with clear ordering based on dependencies.

faq.question: Can I customize the output format?
faq.answer: The skill follows a standard markdown template. You can request specific sections or formatting in your prompt.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This is a prompt-based skill. It only reads project files for analysis and writes plans to your local .claude/plans/ directory.

faq.question: Why is my plan not being saved?
faq.answer: Ensure you have permission to write to the .claude/plans/ directory. The skill requires explicit user approval before writing files.

faq.question: How is this different from just asking Claude?
faq.answer: This skill enforces a systematic planning methodology including requirements gathering, codebase analysis, and structured task breakdown.
